entry.title: Re: How to read environment variable of project of SAP Build Process Automation in Script Task
entry.content:
@weiss_zhao1 Can you describe your scenario a little more? I am assuming you have an automation with environment variables that you then want to use in the main process?

Environment variables are used with automations: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-environment-variable-06185612f8c14b08a977aed8553f438a?version=Cloud&q=environment+variables 

If you'd want this variables in your process, you can easily create outputs in your automation and set them to the environment variables.

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content: i am returning from TechEd in UK, but I will investigate on my return … but could you use the regular set variable flow function in the meantime?

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content: You mean using a standard "set variable" flow function?

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content: @nicholas1959 Maybe you can explain your use case, and how you set it up technically. I was able to use "Set repeated item value" without a problem. Please explain the entire flow and when it does not work.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process: How to check if action returned re...
entry.content:
You can certainly do this now with script task, one of its main use cases is working with lists.

unless someone does this before, when I return later today I will give example

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Components Simultaneously in SAP Build Apps’ Logic Canvas?
entry.content:
Hi @Ricardo2, it is just before the Sabbath so I can;t take a big look at this now -- I will tomorrow night.

1) Usually if you wanted to turn on and off components, you would use a page variable and bind it to the Visible property of the component, or if you want to control different components separately, create several variables. Is that viable in your case?

2) You said you could not create more than 1 input property. You mean for the Hide/Show Component flow function, or did you mean something else?

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Components Simultaneously in SAP Build Apps’ Logic Canvas?
entry.content:
1) You can set a page variable to visible property. When you do, there is a setting for a preview value. So you can have a page variable initially set to false, but when you bind it to the visible property, you can set the preview value to true, and the component will be visible in design time.

2) They haven't exposed formulas for show/hide or for setting a component property, and they haven't let you specify a group of components for these flow functions, so I don't know how you would do it. 😾

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process: How to check if action returned re...
entry.content:
Yes, there are lists, and if you want you can find out how many items are in the list with a script task. 

Let's say I create a trigger form and it has a table of names. I can write a script to retrieve the list and then calculate how many items there are. I could then return the value in a custom variable.

var mylist = $.context.startEvent.namesTable;
$.context.custom.count = mylist.length;

entry.title: Re: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content: https://github.com/sap-tutorials/sap-build-apps/tree/main/projects/google-scrape-images-automation

entry.title: Re: REVIEW: New Layout / Features for SAP Build Apps
entry.content: I'm sorry but I do not understand exactly which properties you are changing and how it appears in design time and preview. Again, I added a dropdown and previewed it and all was OK.

entry.title: Re: How to create code list field in sap build process au...
entry.content:
This seems like a perfect case of a decision.

In a form you can use the choice field, and then use a decision to take this value and output the cost. Then display the cost in a form or call an action if you need to store it externally.

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content:
1) When the user clicks the text, then the item disappears? Can a user make it come back? Can you provide some screenshots?

2) The formula above sets the visibility to true, no? Don't you want to set to false?

3) Did you send me somehow your project? Your last reply suggests I have access to it.

entry.title: Re: How to create code list field in sap build process au...
entry.content: Not sure what the objection is. You are storing those values in SAP Build Process Automation, in a Decision that lives in your process automation project. Not only that but any app could call that decision via API and get the cost from the tool string. In addition, you could store the selected value in a custom variable, and you could store the array of tools/cost pairs in a custom variable -- you could use a script task if you wanted to avoid a decision task and select the correct cost from the input value for the tool. Depends what your end goal here is.

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content:
I created an app connected to Northwind's Employee entity. I created a container that is repeated with the Employees, and then have a container inside that is displayed or hidden based on the Extension field. A text field can be clicked to hide/show that container.

The logic for the Hide/Show text is a "Set repeated item value" flow function.

And here are the properties for the flow function:

I just set the extension to 1 to display, and to empty string to hide. 

I'm sure there must have been a change (I will alert dev), but to help find where the change was made, I am trying to isolate when the "bug" occurs and when it doesn't occur.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Previewing SAP Build Apps on Mobile
entry.content:
What region of BTP are you in?

The most common reason for this is that you clicked on the big "SAP Build Apps" button (which is only for EU10) and you are on US10. If this is the reason, you have to click on "Other login options".

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content:
I think Dev would have to be certain there was a bug, and they would have to know the difference between what I created and what you created so they can find out what use case does the flow function fail at, if at all. 

Also, I'm not sure you said what landscape you are using.

The only interesting this is that apparently this flow function does not modify the data in the variable, but only in the UI.

entry.title: Re: validate an input field in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Use formula like this:

 

IF(MATCHES_REGEX("1234567890123a", "^[0-9]{12}$"),"MATCH","BOOOOOOOOOOOO!")

 

 Obviously, replaces the "123..." string with your variable.

P.S.: Thanks, ChatGPT 😺

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Error while passing payload using postman - p...
entry.content: @arroyoweisson Did you need something?

entry.title: Santa builds process to distribute Christmas presents
entry.content:
No one would deny that Santa has a complex process to give out presents for Christmas. 

But several key new SAP Build Process Automation features has enabled Santa to automate the process using a true Fusion Team of reindeer, elves, and his wife. Among the features:

  • Scheduled triggers
  • Script task
  • Custom variables
  • Subprocesses
  • Go to task

And the process can actually teach us some of the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation.

Scheduled Triggers

Probably the most obvious feature to help Santa is the scheduled trigger for regular processes. We could rely on one of the elves to send an API call, but the scheduled trigger is much more reliable.

Action and Data Type to Get Children 

The process uses an action to retrieves a list of children.

We first created a data type to represent a child (name string and a Boolean on whether they were nice or naughty).

Then we created custom variables, one to hold the full list of children, and another to hold the current child in our iteration.

Then we run an action to get the children.

Script Task and GoTo for Iterating Children

Now we create a script task to move the last child into the current child variable, and to remove that child from the full list.

And finally we create an iteration sequence by a Go To back to the start, where we have a condition to see if the list has more children, and 

Subprocesses

We create subprocesses like "Prepare Reindeer and Sled" since we are likely to need to do that during the year, and we create subprocesses like "Deliver via Chimney" in order to simplify and isolate our logic.

Obvious, in a more realistic case we would have created additional logic to review all the actions of a child to determine if they were nice or naughty, as Santa "sees you when you're sleepin'" and "knows when you're awake", and likely is aware of all actions of a children, so we might use a Decision to make that determination.

And we might create an automation to deal with the complexity of building the toys, handling emails in Outlook from kids wanting presents, and looking for sales on toys on the web. 

🎅🎄❄🤶⛪🕎🕌🤗🦌

Hoping the holiday season and the new year brings you peace and love and happiness, and everything you dream about.

entry.title: Re: validate an input field in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
The error message explains the issue.

i assume you have an IF flow function, so you just need to return Boolean. You just need the MATCH_REGEX without the IF function
